title: Method of forming headrest stay
abstract: Disclosed is a method of forming a headrest stay which easily performs a bending process so that edges of both ends of the headrest stay have curvature radii of 5 mm or more by chamfering outer surfaces of the both ends of the headrest stay.
claims: Claims ( 3 ) A method of forming a headrest stay, comprising: (a) providing a headrest stay having a pipe shape; (b) chamfering an outer surface of a first chamfered section formed throughout a certain section in a longitudinal direction from an end of the headrest stay so that a thickness of the headrest stay at the first chamfered section is decreased in a direction toward the end of the headrest stay; (c) chamfering an outer surface of a second chamfered section formed throughout a certain section in a longitudinal direction from another end of the headrest stay so that the thickness of the headrest stay at the second chamfered section is decreased in a direction toward the other end of the headrest stay; (e) bending an end part of the headrest stay toward a center in a radial direction; and (f) bending another end part of the headrest stay toward a center in a radial direction. The method of claim 1, further comprising (d) removing processing chips, which move into the headrest stay, from the headrest stay by injecting air into the headrest stay, wherein the operation (d) is performed between operations (c) and (e). The method of claim 1, wherein in operation (e), a curvature radius of the end part of the headrest stay at the outer surface is 5 mm or more, and wherein in operation (f), a curvature radius of the other end part of the headrest stay at the outer surface is 5 mm or more.
